message.content: {"top_matches":[{"player":"dog-orange-384","probability":0.6},{"player":"cat-pink-8143","probability":0.3},{"player":"buffalo-red-6989","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["Persistent, small-step proposing pattern by dog-orange-384 matches my investigative, methodical side — repeated modest offers that aim for steady gains rather than risky gambits.","dog-orange-384 accepted a mildly favorable trade late in the game, consistent with a pragmatic, data-driven decision to secure a clear positive payoff rather than chase uncertain large wins.","cat-pink-8143’s larger, riskier offers resemble the more experimental, spontaneous aspects of my persona (willingness to overcommit or try bold moves), but those proposals sometimes look suboptimal or impulsive compared with my usual analytic tendencies.","buffalo-red-6989 appears highly opportunistic (very favorable proposer valuations) but passive in securing trades; this fits least with my combination of persistence and strategic acceptance behavior."],"uncertainty_notes":["Only public moves and realized payouts are visible; private valuations drive many accept/decline decisions, so behavioral interpretation is inferred rather than certain.","My own persona blends both analytic caution and artistic risk-taking, so I could plausibly map to either the steady proposer (dog) or the bold proposer (cat); assigned probabilities reflect that ambiguity.","Random selection when multiple acceptances occur and small sample of accepted trades (one executed) limit confidence in precise match."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"whale-green-1798","probability":0.7},{"player":"swan-pink-1424","probability":0.2},{"player":"turtle-yellow-7601","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["whale-green-1798 consistently proposes trades aligned with a clear valuation strategy (offers GREEN to buy RED), adapts proposal size after an initial large/ambitious offer, and selectively accepts only profitable incoming offers — matching a data-driven, strategic, leadership-oriented approach.","swan-pink-1424 is opportunistic and creative in structuring offers that extract value (offering low-value RED to acquire GREEN) and successfully negotiates beneficial trades, but also accepts a clearly loss-making deal once — reflecting occasional spontaneity or overcommitment.","turtle-yellow-7601 behaves cautiously: repeatedly proposes favorable-but-rejected bundles and accepts only clearly beneficial small swaps. This conservative, less proactive pattern aligns least with the journalist’s iterative leadership and networking tendencies."],"uncertainty_notes":["Behavior is inferred from a short sequence of economic choices in a stylized game; real-world personality mapping is noisy.","Private chip valuations strongly drive actions here, so some moves reflect payoff incentives more than temperament.","Random selection when multiple acceptances occur and unobserved decision reasoning (e.g., mistakes, experimentation) introduce ambiguity in attributing motives."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"buffalo-orange-6610","probability":0.6},{"player":"monkey-pink-6874","probability":0.3},{"player":"goose-purple-9985","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["buffalo-orange-6610: Consistently proposes and accepts trades that increase their payoff, declines unfavorable deals—matches an analytical, data-driven, strategic style consistent with my investigative/journalistic persona.","monkey-pink-6874: Makes creative/ambitious offers and sometimes accepts unfavorable trades—reflects the experimental spontaneity and occasional overcommitment in my persona.","goose-purple-9985: Mixed behavior (some profitable proposals but also acceptance of a loss); less consistent with my profile than the other two."],"uncertainty_notes":["I don’t have my own private chip values, so mapping observed moves to my likely internal incentives is inferential.","Small number of rounds and random selection when multiple players accept can obscure intent—some accepted trades may reflect being chosen rather than deliberate preference.","Players may be experimenting or making mistakes; behavioral match is probabilistic rather than definitive."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"ram-pink-8623","probability":0.6},{"player":"squid-yellow-4597","probability":0.3},{"player":"otter-pink-5988","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["ram-pink-8623: Varied, sometimes generous proposals and frequent acceptance of trades (including ones that reduce payout) fit an experimental, eclectic decision style — aligns with Anna’s artistic spontaneity, willingness to try different approaches, and occasional overcommitment.","squid-yellow-4597: Consistent, value-seeking proposals and selective acceptance reflect the data-driven, investigative side of Anna’s persona — disciplined, strategic, and profit-focused.","otter-pink-5988: Opportunistic and occasionally strategic (profitable proposals) but less consistently aligned with Anna’s blend of creativity and investigative rigor; more of a secondary match."],"uncertainty_notes":["Behavior inferred from a small number of rounds — actions may reflect short-term testing rather than stable personality.","Game choices reflect private chip values and opportunistic incentives; we observe behavior but not internal motives, so mapping to a rich real-world persona is probabilistic.","Anna’s profile blends creativity and analytical rigor, so behaviors matching either side (exploratory vs. strictly profit-maximizing) could plausibly represent her decisions."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"camel-pink-767","probability":0.65},{"player":"alligator-orange-9612","probability":0.25},{"player":"butterfly-yellow-2139","probability":0.10}],"behavioral_basis":["camel-pink-767 consistently proposes trades that increase their own payout by acquiring the higher‑value chip (RED) and makes occasional larger, riskier offers that get declined — matches Anna’s data‑driven but experimental, occasionally overcommitting style.","alligator-orange-9612 behaves like a pragmatic, opportunistic trader: repeatedly offers low‑value RED to buy GREEN (profitable given their private values) and accepts favorable incoming offers — aligns with Anna’s investigative, strategic side but less of her creative/experimenting streak.","butterfly-yellow-2139 shows inconsistent, self‑hurting proposals (offering GREEN to buy RED despite valuing RED more) and mainly accepts only clearly favorable offers — this behavior is less consistent with Anna’s careful, analytical decision‑making and her tendency to experiment thoughtfully."],"uncertainty_notes":["Only three rounds (nine proposals) observed — small sample size for strong inference.","Private chip values drive much behavior; matching relies on inferred decision logic rather than rich personality signals.","Some proposals (e.g., large declined offers) could reflect mistakes, misclicks, or bluffing rather than stable personality traits, increasing ambiguity."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"owl-yellow-7660","probability":0.7},{"player":"iguana-red-1137","probability":0.2},{"player":"whale-blue-4588","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["owl-yellow-7660 used a steady, repeatable small‑trade strategy (offering 2 GREEN for 3 RED each proposer turn) that produced reliable incremental gains — matching my data‑driven, iterative approach to testing offers and preferring modest, understandable bets over flashy gambits.","iguana-red-1137 made large, creative/high‑stake proposals (big GREEN offers for many RED) that were frequently declined — this resembles my creative spontaneity and occasional tendency to overcommit or try bold experiments, even if they sometimes fail.","whale-blue-4588’s play was more opportunistic and inconsistent (mixing offers and sometimes accepting others’ proposals), which fits a pragmatic, short‑term opportunist style but less closely with my investigative, structured decisionmaking. "],"uncertainty_notes":["Inference is based on a small number of rounds and observed proposals; behavior could be noise or experimental rather than stable personality signal.","Private chip valuations and the random selection rule for multiple acceptances affect choices in ways that aren’t fully observable, so some acceptance/decline decisions may reflect strategic concealment or risk preferences not directly tied to personality.","Players may be pursuing payoff-maximizing strategies specific to their private values rather than expressing stable social or personality traits; this limits confidence in mapping game moves to real‑world persona."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"duck-pink-5390","probability":0.60},{"player":"gorilla-purple-6898","probability":0.30},{"player":"kangaroo-purple-4743","probability":0.10}],"behavioral_basis":["Duck repeatedly makes bold, creative offers (trading many low‑value REDs for GREENS), consistent with Anna’s tendency to leverage cheap resources for higher‑value outcomes and experiment with eclectic strategies.","Duck shows selective acceptance and occasional opportunism—mirroring Anna’s mix of spontaneity and strategic judgment rather than a purely rigid or purely exploitative style.","Gorilla’s repetitive, modest‑gain proposals map to Anna’s investigative, data‑driven side (systematic, low‑risk), so they are a secondary match.","Kangaroo’s strongly self‑interested, opportunistic exploitation of high RED value is less aligned with Anna’s collaborative, creative‑risk profile, so it’s the least likely match."],"uncertainty_notes":["Observed behavior is driven by private chip valuations (not personality) so some actions reflect incentives rather than individual traits.","Small number of rounds and repeated, valuation‑driven proposals limit confidence in inferring stable personality traits from this game.","Anna’s profile mixes analytical and creative traits, which produces ambiguity between Duck (creative risk‑taking) and Gorilla (systematic, analytical)."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"camel-pink-767","probability":0.75},{"player":"alligator-orange-9612","probability":0.20},{"player":"butterfly-yellow-2139","probability":0.05}],"behavioral_basis":["Consistently accepted and proposed trades that exploit comparative-value differences (willing to give GREEN to obtain high‑value RED and accept offers that exchange GREEN for RED) — aligns with an analytical, payoff‑maximizing approach.","Proposals were modest and practical rather than extreme; traded when mutually beneficial signals were clear, consistent with a data‑driven, strategic decision maker.","Alligator shows some similar opportunistic behavior but is less consistently proactive; Butterfly’s repeated unrealistic/overreaching proposals suggest a more erratic/greedy style, unlike the measured experimentalism in the persona."],"uncertainty_notes":["Only a small number of rounds and actions observed — behavioral inference is tentative.","Players’ private information and possible off‑game motives (e.g., experimental or signaling behavior) could explain anomalous moves (e.g., camel’s one proposal offering RED for GREEN).","Probabilities reflect relative fit to the described persona, not certainty about identity — alternative interpretations are possible given limited data."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"turtle-yellow-3106","probability":0.62},{"player":"elephant-yellow-5788","probability":0.23},{"player":"pig-blue-1277","probability":0.15}],"behavioral_basis":["turtle-yellow-3106 repeatedly made bold, generous proposals that created clear mutual gains and successfully closed two trades — this aligns with Anna’s proactive, collaborative, risk-tolerant creative streak and tendency to pursue high-impact, win–win initiatives.","elephant-yellow-5788 behaved opportunistically and analytically: declined unattractive offers, accepted mutually profitable ones, and proposed modest offers that failed — consistent with Anna’s investigative, data-driven side that looks for clear value but can be conservative in proposals.","pig-blue-1277 repeatedly proposed trades that were declined, suggesting a more experimental or less calibrated approach; this matches Anna’s willingness to try creative/novel strategies but less so her adaptive, data-informed side."],"uncertainty_notes":["Game reveals only a few rounds of behavior — limited data to infer personality reliably.","Players’ private chip values (and full motivations) shape choices; mapping those incentives to real-world personality is imprecise.","Random selection when multiple acceptances occur and strategic considerations about being chosen may have influenced responses in ways not observable here."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"bat-purple-4553","probability":0.6},{"player":"tiger-yellow-3019","probability":0.3},{"player":"unicorn-pink-1559","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["bat-purple-4553: Opportunistic and payoff‑focused (accepted trades that clearly increased their payout, made modest proposals), matching an analytical, data‑driven, adaptive approach consistent with my journalist/problem‑solving persona.","tiger-yellow-3019: Persistent, methodical, and conservative (repeated small, sensible offers to acquire higher‑value RED chips) which aligns with patient investigative habits and steady experimentation.","unicorn-pink-1559: Risk‑taking and inconsistent (large, sometimes unfavorable deals and accepting poor offers), showing more impulsive/experimental behavior than my typical measured, analytic style."],"uncertainty_notes":["Only in‑game bargaining behavior is observed; broader personality inference is limited.","Random selection when multiple acceptors exist can obscure true preferences and motivations.","Players’ private incentives (their chip valuations) drive many choices; behavior may reflect simple payoff maximization rather than deeper personality traits."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"buffalo-green-2230","probability":0.65},{"player":"bear-green-5745","probability":0.25},{"player":"goose-green-3433","probability":0.10}],"behavioral_basis":["Buffalo crafted proposals that appeared tailored to others’ incentives (e.g., offering GREEN to buy BLUE) and selectively accepted only mutually beneficial trades—matching an analytical, social‑strategic approach consistent with a journalist who models others’ preferences.","Bear shows straightforward, value‑maximizing behavior (targeting BLUE aggressively and accepting clear gains), which aligns with a pragmatic, data‑driven side of my persona but is less exploratory than Buffalo.","Goose is single‑mindedly pursuing GREEN and repeatedly proposes the same type of trade; this is opportunistic but less reflective of the cooperative, investigative, and audience‑aware stance I’d adopt."],"uncertainty_notes":["Behavioral signals are limited to a small number of rounds and discrete proposals; some choices (accept/decline) may reflect private randomization or risk preferences not observable here.","Random selection when multiple acceptances occur and unobserved motives (e.g., testing strategies or signaling) could make surface actions misrepresent underlying disposition."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"cat-green-4185","probability":0.7},{"player":"iguana-yellow-3262","probability":0.2},{"player":"zebra-pink-2457","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["Frequent, successful proposals and varied offers — consistent with a proactive, strategic actor who experiments with different trades while maintaining good outcomes (matches Anna’s investigative + creative but disciplined style).","Iguana shows cooperative behavior and occasional risk-taking (learns by trading but sometimes overreaches), reflecting some exploratory, adaptive tendencies of Anna’s persona but less consistently strategic.","Zebra’s play is more erratic and sometimes self-damaging (proposing trades that reduce their own payout), which aligns with spontaneous creativity but not with the disciplined, data-driven judgment emphasized in Anna’s profile."],"uncertainty_notes":["Only three rounds of play observed — limited sample of behavioral patterns.","Players may be experimenting or role‑playing, so motives (strategic vs. random) are not fully identifiable from actions alone.","Private valuations and random tie-breaking can produce outcomes that obscure a player’s intended strategy."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"badger-green-4722","probability":0.60},{"player":"peacock-purple-5231","probability":0.30},{"player":"unicorn-blue-7096","probability":0.10}],"behavioral_basis":["Badger repeatedly proposed straightforward, mutually beneficial trades (offer greens to buy blues) and persisted when it worked — consistent with a pragmatic, analytical collaborator who values clear win-win outcomes (aligns with a journalist’s data-driven, cooperative approach).","Peacock showed opportunistic, goal-directed behavior (focused on acquiring high-value reds), accepted mutually beneficial offers, and sometimes pushed for larger gains — matching Anna’s ambitious, experimental streak though with more aggressive risk-taking.","Unicorn behaved more selectively and sometimes declined cooperative offers; their pattern is more self-maximizing and inconsistent, which fits Anna least given her collaborative, investigative orientation."],"uncertainty_notes":["Inferences are based only on visible proposals and accept/decline decisions; private motives and off-record reasoning aren’t observable.","The sample of rounds is small and some acceptances were randomly resolved when multiple players accepted, which can obscure intentional partner choice.","Players’ private chip valuations strongly shape behavior; matching personality to strategic moves involves interpretation and could be mistaken if a player was following a hidden payoff-driven plan rather than a stable social/psychological style."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"peacock-red-3668","probability":0.7},{"player":"cat-orange-2462","probability":0.2},{"player":"otter-blue-7516","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["Peacock consistently proposed trades that clearly maximized proposer payoff (offering lower‑value chips to buy higher‑value RED), accepted selectively, and realized the largest gains — matching an analytical, strategic approach consistent with Anna’s data‑driven, results‑oriented persona.","Cat often accepted small or neutral‑payoff trades and made modestly positive proposals, showing cooperative flexibility and willingness to pursue incremental gains — aligning with Anna’s collaborative, creative multitasking tendencies (but less decisively strategic).","Otter proposed profitable trades for themselves but sometimes miscalibrated offers (large proposals that others declined), suggesting risk‑taking creativity but less precise calibration to opponents’ incentives than Anna would likely display."],"uncertainty_notes":["Behavioral inferences are from a small number of rounds (limited sample) and may not reflect stable strategy.","Players in the game observe only their own private values; observed accept/decline behavior could reflect experimentation, risk preferences, or misreading of opponents rather than stable personality.","Random selection among multiple acceptors and unobserved motives (e.g., social preferences) introduce additional ambiguity in mapping game actions to real‑world personality."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"otter-yellow-9944","probability":0.55},{"player":"bear-blue-8468","probability":0.35},{"player":"dog-red-9959","probability":0.10}],"behavioral_basis":["Otter is an active, creative proposer who repeatedly constructs deals and targets specific partners—matches my tendency to drive interactions, try experimental offers, and cultivate cooperative exchanges.","Bear behaves like a careful, data-driven decision‑maker who accepts only clearly beneficial trades and declines marginal/poor offers—aligns with my investigative, analytic side and selectivity about collaborations.","Dog shows riskier, inconsistent acceptances and made bold proposals that often failed—this experimental but sometimes suboptimal style is least like my usual calculated approach."],"uncertainty_notes":["Inferences are based only on observed offers/acceptances and chip payouts; actual motives or private valuations might lead to different behavioral interpretations.","Players may have strategic reasons (e.g., hiding preferences, signaling) that diverge from surface-level patterns; matching is probabilistic, not definitive.","My real-world persona mixes creativity with analysis, so some behaviors (active proposing vs. highly selective acceptance) point to different players and produce uncertainty in ranking."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"ram-purple-8899","probability":0.56},{"player":"owl-yellow-9160","probability":0.28},{"player":"lion-yellow-9203","probability":0.16}],"behavioral_basis":["Frequent, proactive proposer (leads rounds) — matches an energetic, multitasking personality that initiates exchanges and shapes interaction.","Proposals consistently target higher‑value chips for that player (data‑driven, value‑maximizing pattern) consistent with an analytical, investigative approach to tradeoffs.","Occasional acceptance of a net‑loss trade suggests a willingness to take creative or social risks and to overcommit — fits the persona’s tendency to juggle projects and sometimes bend discipline."],"uncertainty_notes":["We only observe revealed proposals and accept/decline choices, not motivations—private chip values largely determine ‘rational’ moves and can mask personality signals.","Some single anomalous decisions (e.g., accepting a net loss) could be random/noise or result from risk preferences not fully aligned with the persona; sample size is small (3 rounds).","Random selection when multiple acceptances occur and other strategic considerations (e.g., scarcity concerns, planned reciprocity) introduce ambiguity in attributing intentions."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"butterfly-purple-4350","probability":0.75},{"player":"owl-yellow-8687","probability":0.20},{"player":"ram-green-9746","probability":0.05}],"behavioral_basis":["Frequent, varied proposals and willingness to both propose and accept trades — matches Anna’s proactive, experimental, multitasking, creative style (butterfly made the most diverse offers and engaged often).","Targets high‑value assets aggressively but with clear focus (owl repeatedly tried to acquire BLUE and succeeded with a decisive later offer) — this maps to Anna’s data‑driven, goal‑oriented side, though less playful than butterfly.","More narrowly focused, steady strategy to obtain a single preferred chip (ram’s repeated PURPLE‑seeking proposals) reflects investigative persistence but lacks the spontaneous, artsy risk‑taking characteristic of Anna, so lower match."],"uncertainty_notes":["Behavioral inference is based on a small number of rounds and publicly visible actions; private valuations and random partner selection can obscure intent.","Players’ observed choices may reflect short‑term opportunism or inventory constraints rather than stable personality traits.","Some behaviors (proposing often vs. single‑minded proposing) could be produced by different personalities under game incentives; probabilities express this ambiguity."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"buffalo-yellow-4552","probability":0.7},{"player":"goose-green-9189","probability":0.2},{"player":"bear-red-8738","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["High proposal frequency and persistence: buffalo repeatedly proposed across rounds, matching Anna’s proactive, multi-project drive and willingness to keep experimenting after declines.","Creative, opportunistic trades: buffalo’s initial successful offer (trading low‑value RED for high‑value BLUE) and continued novel offers align with Anna’s tendency to pursue bold, unconventional strategies.","Acceptance of mutually beneficial deals: goose accepted a clearly profitable trade, which fits Anna’s data‑driven decision making but goose’s subsequent attempts to extract value feel more purely opportunistic than Anna’s collaborative streak.","Caution vs. risk: bear’s frequent declines and conservative responses align least with Anna’s experimental, outgoing persona."],"uncertainty_notes":["Actions observed are few (three rounds) and may not capture full strategy or personality.","Private chip valuations were visible in the record but would not have been known to players during actual play; matching behavior to persona is inferential.","Players’ in‑game motives could be strategic (e.g., misleading, testing) rather than reflecting dispositional personality traits."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"camel-yellow-4406","probability":0.65},{"player":"badger-purple-8825","probability":0.25},{"player":"otter-orange-6095","probability":0.10}],"behavioral_basis":["camel-yellow-4406 made a variety of exploratory proposals across different chip colors (RED→BLUE, PURPLE→BLUE, BLUE→PURPLE), suggesting experimental, creative play consistent with an eclectic, spontaneity-oriented persona; accepted a neutral trade, indicating flexibility and willingness to try social/cooperative moves.","badger-purple-8825 proposed targeted, value‑seeking trades (e.g., offering RED to buy PURPLE; GREEN→BLUE) showing investigative/strategic tendencies and occasional willingness to accept partner proposals, consistent with a methodical but curious actor.","otter-orange-6095 pursued consistent, pragmatic offers using low‑value RED to acquire other chips and captured clear gains—efficient and profit‑focused rather than exploratory, which fits less well with the artistic/experimental profile."],"uncertainty_notes":["Observed play covers only a few rounds — limited window to infer stable personality-aligned patterns.","Players’ private valuations are known here but their motivations (risk tolerance, social preferences, attention) are not directly observable and can produce similar moves for different reasons.","Random selection when multiple acceptances, and occasional single acceptance choices, can obscure whether actions were strategic or opportunistic."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"dog-orange-384","probability":0.7},{"player":"buffalo-red-6989","probability":0.25},{"player":"cat-pink-8143","probability":0.05}],"behavioral_basis":["Consistently proposed modest, self‑beneficial trades (small amounts of RED to buy GREEN) and declined offers that were value‑negative — matches an analytical, risk‑averse negotiator.","Accepted only the one trade that yielded a clear positive payoff (+$0.20), showing cautious opportunism rather than aggressive risk‑taking.","Did not make large, risky offers that would harm own payout (unlike the player who accepted a loss), reflecting careful, data‑driven decision style similar to a procurement professional."],"uncertainty_notes":["Inference drawn solely from a short, stylized chip‑bargaining interaction; real‑world personality signals may differ.","Players’ private chip valuations strongly drive behavior here; similarity may reflect payoff structure rather than stable personal traits.","Probabilities express relative behavioral fit in this game, not a definitive measure of overall personality alignment."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"otter-blue-1497","probability":0.70},{"player":"badger-red-827","probability":0.20},{"player":"peacock-pink-777","probability":0.10}],"behavioral_basis":["Made modest, repeatable offers and targeted likely accepting partners (consistent with a strategic, data-driven negotiator).","Balanced proposing and accepting trades when they increased payout, showing pragmatic decision‑making rather than risky gambles.","Avoided extreme or impulsive proposals (unlike the large, unlikely offer from peacock), aligning with a reserved but assertive purchasing style."],"uncertainty_notes":["Only observed actions in a short, stylized bargaining game — behavior may not fully reflect broader personality.","Players’ private chip valuations drive choices; without direct mapping from my real‑world preferences to chip values, inference is approximate.","Random tie‑breaking when multiple acceptances occurred and limited sample of rounds add noise to behavioral interpretation."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"squid-yellow-4597","probability":0.7},{"player":"otter-pink-5988","probability":0.2},{"player":"ram-pink-8623","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["Consistently proposes trades that increase their own payout (offers lower-valued RED to acquire higher-valued GREEN), reflecting analytical, self‑interested bargaining and strategic play consistent with a procurement professional focused on value.","Regularly accepts deals that are clearly profitable to them and declines those that are not, showing disciplined selection of opportunities rather than overly generous or erratic behavior.","Otter shows similar self‑interest but is less consistent in responses; Ram often makes proposals that advantage others, which conflicts with a calculated, efficiency‑oriented approach."],"uncertainty_notes":["Assignment based on limited, short game history (9 proposals) — patterns could shift over longer play.","Private acceptances and random tie resolution introduce noise that may mask intent; observed proposals and realized payoffs are proxies for strategy, not direct statements of personality.","My interpretation assumes Elizabeth Weaver would prioritize analytically optimal, moderately assertive offers rather than generous or highly variable play."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"ram-red-5745","probability":0.7},{"player":"dolphin-purple-6605","probability":0.2},{"player":"iguana-orange-9224","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["ram-red-5745 repeatedly proposed trades that captured surplus for the proposer while structuring offers likely acceptable to iguana-orange-9224 (consistent, strategic offer design and successful negotiation), matching an analytical, negotiation‑oriented style (procurement agent).","dolphin-purple-6605 behaved cautiously: frequently declined unfavorable proposals and only accepted a neutral trade, which maps to a risk‑averse, reserved approach but less proactively strategic than the target persona.","iguana-orange-9224 made proposals that exploited asymmetric valuations (proactive and opportunistic) and accepted beneficial offers, but also executed one clearly value‑reducing trade—suggesting occasional miscalculation rather than the consistently data‑driven, deliberate behavior expected from the described persona."],"uncertainty_notes":["Small number of rounds and offers — behavior may not fully reveal underlying decision rules or longer‑term strategy.","Private chip values (visible to us) influenced incentives; real players’ reasoning could include bounded rationality, experimentation, or mistakes not reflecting steady personality.","Random selection when multiple accepters or unobserved off‑game factors (e.g., misunderstandings) could skew which behaviors appear dominant."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"owl-yellow-7660","probability":0.7},{"player":"iguana-red-1137","probability":0.2},{"player":"whale-blue-4588","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["Consistent, modest proposals and steady, risk‑aware acceptance behavior (owl) match my strategic, analytical, incremental negotiation style — repeated small profitable trades show methodical optimization rather than flashy gambits.","Iguana’s behavior shows strong profitability focus and several bold offers (large green-for-red proposals). That aligns with my negotiation drive and willingness to propose deals, but the frequent large/overgenerous offers feel less restrained and less likely for my cautious, data‑driven style.","Whale’s opportunistic pattern of accepting others’ small offers and making mixed proposals looks more opportunistic and a bit erratic compared with my reserved, planful approach; I’d be less likely to propose moves that look internally inconsistent."],"uncertainty_notes":["Only a small number of rounds are observed; players’ short-run tactics can mask longer-term strategic preferences.","We only observe revealed moves and outcomes, not their reasoning; some profitable but seemingly ‘bold’ proposals could still reflect careful calculation under different risk preferences.","Random selection when multiple players accept and limited proposal space make some inferences probabilistic rather than definitive."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"cat-green-4185","probability":0.72},{"player":"iguana-yellow-3262","probability":0.20},{"player":"zebra-pink-2457","probability":0.08}],"behavioral_basis":["cat-green-4185 repeatedly made clear, data-consistent profitable proposals (e.g., offering lower-valued chips to buy higher-valued RED) and accepted trades that improved payout — reflecting analytical, efficiency-focused, risk-aware behavior consistent with a strategic purchasing agent.","iguana-yellow-3262 pursued opportunistic, value-extractive offers and accepted reasonable trades but sometimes pushed aggressive terms (one declined round), suggesting a pragmatic negotiator who is less consistently conservative than the top match.","zebra-pink-2457 showed more variability: a couple of highly profitable moves but also a notable self-harming proposal (resulting in a loss), indicating riskier or less-calibrated play unlike the steady, data-driven style expected from the persona."],"uncertainty_notes":["Only a small number of rounds and proposals observed — behavioral inferences are noisy.","Private chip values and random selection when multiple acceptors exist affect choices and observed outcomes; we can’t observe players’ internal decision rules or preferences beyond revealed moves.","Players may be experimenting, retaliating, or reacting to perceived intentions (social factors) rather than strictly optimizing monetary payoff, which could confound the match."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"dog-green-8079","probability":0.65},{"player":"parrot-pink-5400","probability":0.25},{"player":"turtle-red-9966","probability":0.10}],"behavioral_basis":["dog-green-8079 made conservative, repeatable, small-profit proposals and targeted a predictable partner — reflects a methodical, data-driven negotiator who prefers steady gains (matches a purchasing agent’s cautious, strategic style).","parrot-pink-5400 proposed large, highly profitable trades and succeeded at extracting value — shows strong negotiation instincts and willingness to make bold moves (partially aligns with an assertive procurement professional, but is more aggressive/risk-taking than my persona).","turtle-red-9966 frequently proposed deals that others declined and accepted offers that reduced their payout — this inconsistent, occasionally self‑damaging behavior is less like the careful, analytical approach I would take."],"uncertainty_notes":["We observe only three rounds with limited context; behavior could be influenced by private valuations or off‑record strategy (e.g., signaling, mistakes).","Random selection when multiple players accept and unobserved private motives can obscure true strategy alignment.","Player actions might reflect experimental or deceptive play that doesn’t map perfectly onto real‑world professional personalities."]}

{"top_matches":[{"player":"cat-orange-2462","probability":0.6},{"player":"peacock-red-3668","probability":0.3},{"player":"otter-blue-7516","probability":0.1}],"behavioral_basis":["Frequent, modest trades and many acceptances suggest a cooperative, risk‑aware style consistent with a reserved but practical negotiator.","Proposals by cat are pragmatic and incremental (small offers that yield steady, modest gains) matching an analytical, relationship‑oriented approach.","Peacock shows more aggressive, opportunistic moves and larger gains — somewhat assertive and strategic, plausible as a secondary match to my negotiation strengths.","Otter’s behavior is more hit‑or‑miss and sometimes overreaching (offering low‑value chips for high‑value ones), which fits less well with a cautious, data‑driven persona."],"uncertainty_notes":["Only a few rounds are observed, so behavior may not fully represent each player’s typical style.","Player chip valuations and random selection when multiple acceptances occur can obscure intent behind some proposals/acceptances.","My inference maps game actions to personality traits, which is inherently approximate and subject to alternative plausible interpretations."]}
